questions.question: What is gravitation?
questions.answer: Gravitation is the force of attraction between any two objects in the universe.

questions.question: Who discovered the universal law of gravitation?
questions.answer: Isaac Newton discovered the universal law of gravitation.

questions.question: State the universal law of gravitation.
questions.answer: Every object in the universe attracts every other object with a force proportional to the product of their masses and inversely proportional to the square of the distance between them.

questions.question: Write the formula for gravitational force.
questions.answer: The formula for gravitational force is F = G(Mm)/d².

questions.question: What is free fall?
questions.answer: Free fall is the motion of an object falling under the influence of gravity alone.

questions.question: What is acceleration due to gravity?
questions.answer: Acceleration due to gravity is the acceleration gained by an object due to Earth's gravitational force.

questions.question: What is the value of acceleration due to gravity on Earth?
questions.answer: The value of acceleration due to gravity on Earth is approximately 9.8 m/s².

questions.question: Define mass.
questions.answer: Mass is the amount of matter present in an object.

questions.question: Define weight.
questions.answer: Weight is the force with which the Earth attracts an object.

questions.question: Write the formula for weight.
questions.answer: The formula for weight is W = m × g.

questions.question: What is the SI unit of mass?
questions.answer: The SI unit of mass is kilogram (kg).

questions.question: What is the SI unit of weight?
questions.answer: The SI unit of weight is newton (N).

questions.question: What is thrust?
questions.answer: Thrust is the force acting perpendicular to a surface.

questions.question: Define pressure.
questions.answer: Pressure is the force acting per unit area.

questions.question: Write the formula for pressure.
questions.answer: Pressure = Force / Area.

questions.question: What is the SI unit of pressure?
questions.answer: The SI unit of pressure is Pascal (Pa).

questions.question: What is buoyancy?
questions.answer: Buoyancy is the upward force exerted by a fluid on an object.

questions.question: What is buoyant force?
questions.answer: Buoyant force is the upward force exerted by a fluid on an immersed object.

questions.question: Why do objects float in water?
questions.answer: Objects float when the buoyant force acting on them is equal to or greater than their weight.

questions.question: Why do objects sink in water?
questions.answer: Objects sink when their weight is greater than the buoyant force acting on them.

questions.question: State Archimedes' principle.
questions.answer: When an object is immersed in a fluid, it experiences an upward force equal to the weight of the fluid displaced.

questions.question: Give one application of Archimedes' principle.
questions.answer: Hydrometers used to measure density of liquids are based on Archimedes' principle.

questions.question: Why do ships float on water?
questions.answer: Ships float because the buoyant force acting on them balances their weight.

questions.question: Why does an iron nail sink in water?
questions.answer: An iron nail sinks because its density is greater than the density of water.

questions.question: Name two instruments based on Archimedes' principle.
questions.answer: Hydrometer and lactometer are based on Archimedes' principle.
